children.heading: AIDS
children.label: 1 AIDS
children.text: The term “AIDS” means acquired immune deficiency syndrome.

children.heading: Co-occurring conditions
children.label: 2 Co-occurring conditions
children.text: The term “co-occurring conditions” means one or more adverse health conditions in an individual with HIV/AIDS, without regard to whether the individual has AIDS and without regard to whether the conditions arise from HIV.

children.heading: Counseling
children.label: 3 Counseling
children.text: The term “counseling” means such counseling provided by an individual trained to provide such counseling.

children.heading: Family-centered care
children.label: 4 Family-centered care
children.text: The term “family-centered care” means the system of services described in this subchapter that is targeted specifically to the special needs of infants, children, women and families. Family-centered care shall be based on a partnership between parents, professionals, and the community designed to ensure an integrated, coordinated, culturally sensitive, and community-based continuum of care for children, women, and families with HIV/AIDS.

children.heading: Families with HIV/AIDS
children.label: 5 Families with HIV/AIDS
children.text: The term “families with HIV/AIDS” means families in which one or more members have HIV/AIDS.

children.heading: HIV
children.label: 6 HIV
children.text: The term “HIV” means infection with the human immunodeficiency virus.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “HIV/AIDS” means HIV, and includes AIDS and any condition arising from AIDS.

children.label: i
children.text: the number of living non-AIDS cases of HIV in the area; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of living cases of AIDS in the area.

children.heading: Non-AIDS cases
children.label: C Non-AIDS cases
children.text: The term “non-AIDS”, with respect to a case of HIV, means that the individual involved has HIV but does not have AIDS.

children.heading: Human immunodeficiency virus
children.label: 8 Human immunodeficiency virus
children.text: The term “human immunodeficiency virus” means the etiologic agent for AIDS.

children.heading: Official poverty line
children.label: 9 Official poverty line
children.text: The term “official poverty line” means the poverty line established by the Director of the Office of Management and Budget and revised by the Secretary in accordance with .section 9902(2) of this title

children.heading: Person
children.label: 10 Person
children.text: The term “person” includes one or more individuals, governments (including the Federal Government and the governments of the States), governmental agencies, political subdivisions, labor unions, partnerships, associations, corporations, legal representatives, mutual companies, joint-stock companies, trusts, unincorporated organizations, receivers, trustees, and trustees in cases under title 11.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “State” means each of the 50 States, the District of Columbia, and each of the territories.

children.heading: Territories
children.label: B Territories
children.text: The term “territory” means each of American Samoa, Guam, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, the Virgin Islands, the Republic of the Marshall Islands, the Federated States of Micronesia, and Palau.

children.heading: Youth with HIV
children.label: 12 Youth with HIV
children.text: The term “youth with HIV” means individuals who are 13 through 24 years old and who have HIV/AIDS.
